methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $12M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $539M - $10.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.9x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Colossal Biosciences
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $10.32B

private.name: New Limit
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $3.1B

data.narrative:
Cellares, a Series C-stage biotech company founded by Fabian Gerlinghaus and Omar Kurdi, has raised $350M+ to date. Valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 8 private biotech firms and 3 public comparables in the Biotech & Health sector.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement to a $12M total-raised proxy, implying $20M. Peer-median analysis anchors to four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—Colossal Biosciences ($10.3B), Chai Discovery ($3.8B), New Limit ($3.1B), and Eikon Therapeutics ($539M)—yielding a median valuation of $3.5B across a $539M–$10.3B range. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (6.9x median across RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) cannot be applied due to absence of disclosed revenue.

The triangulated analysis supports a range of $17M (low) to $4.0B (high), with a median of $1.7B. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around stage-appropriate valuation in a frontier-biotech market where peer comparables span nearly two orders of magnitude. Key variables include technology differentiation relative to the peer set and near-term funding trajectory.
